plan.title: Migrate PRISM persistence toward DB-native authoritative runtime storage while keeping snapshots only as derived caches, bootstrap accelerators, and export artifacts, and establish backend-neutral persistence/session abstractions so future Postgres support does not require another architectural rewrite.
plan.goal: Migrate PRISM persistence toward DB-native authoritative runtime storage while keeping snapshots only as derived caches, bootstrap accelerators, and export artifacts, and establish backend-neutral persistence/session abstractions so future Postgres support does not require another architectural rewrite.

node.title: Introduce backend-neutral persistence and session abstractions so SQLite is one backend and future Postgres support can implement the same contract

acceptance.label: Persistence and session contracts describe backend-neutral authoritative state, revision, and hydration capabilities rather than SQLite-specific snapshot mechanics
SQLite-specific construction and runtime assumptions are pushed behind backend implementations or factories instead of leaking through shared orchestration paths
The remaining persistence-migration tasks can proceed without adding new SQLite-only coupling and leave a clear seam for a future Postgres backend

plan.title: Migrate PRISM persistence toward DB-native authoritative runtime storage while keeping snapshots only as derived caches, bootstrap accelerators, and export artifacts, and establish backend-neutral persistence/session abstractions so future Postgres support does not require another architectural rewrite.

plan.title: Migrate PRISM persistence toward backend-neutral, DB-native authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state; keep snapshots and plan compaction artifacts derived-only; and make native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, and compatibility projection explicit persistence concerns rather than a parallel track.
plan.goal: Migrate PRISM persistence toward backend-neutral, DB-native authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state; keep snapshots and plan compaction artifacts derived-only; and make native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, and compatibility projection explicit persistence concerns rather than a parallel track.

node.title: Classify authoritative state vs derived artifacts across persistence, plans, and projections
node.summary: Define what is authoritative runtime truth versus derived or export-only state, including native plan graphs, authored node and edge state, execution overlays, compatibility projections, summaries, and snapshot artifacts.

acceptance.label: Persistence classification explicitly marks native plan graph data, authored node fields, authored edges, execution overlays, and stable published refs as authoritative state where appropriate.
The classification explicitly marks compatibility task projections, plan summaries and recommendations, snapshots, compaction artifacts, and other caches or exports as derived state.
The remaining persistence-migration tasks can reference this classification to avoid reintroducing dual truth between native plans and compatibility projections.

node.title: Introduce backend-neutral persistence and session abstractions for native plans and coordination state
node.summary: Create backend-neutral persistence and session interfaces that cover native plan event storage, snapshot loading, coordination continuity, and binding hydration so SQLite becomes one implementation rather than the architectural center.

acceptance.label: Shared persistence and session contracts cover native plan event storage, native plan snapshot loading, coordination continuity state, and binding hydration or rebinding without SQLite-specific assumptions.
SQLite-specific construction and runtime assumptions are pushed behind backend implementations or factories instead of leaking through shared orchestration paths.
A future Postgres backend can implement the same contracts for plans, memories, concepts, and coordination state without another architectural rewrite.

node.title: Make native plan and coordination writes authoritative via incremental DB-native persistence
node.summary: Replace snapshot-shaped write authority with incremental native writes so plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through authoritative event-backed storage while compatibility projections become sinks rather than semantic write centers.

acceptance.label: Native plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through the authoritative DB-native write path rather than through snapshot-shaped rewrites.
Compatibility task or coordination projections are updated as import or export compatibility views and are no longer treated as the authoritative write truth.
Incremental DB-native writes cover the persistence path for native plan state instead of relying on whole-snapshot rewrites.

node.title: Move outcomes, projections, and plan read models onto incremental maintenance
node.summary: Maintain outcomes, compatibility projections, plan summaries, and recommendation read models incrementally from authoritative events so plan-native state does not require full snapshot rebuilds to become queryable.

acceptance.label: Outcome history, compatibility projections, and plan-native read models such as summaries or next recommendations are maintained incrementally from authoritative events.
Derived read models reflect native plan state without requiring whole-snapshot rebuilds after each mutation or reload.
Projection maintenance clearly distinguishes authoritative event streams from derived read models and exported artifacts.

node.title: Refactor WorkspaceSession hydration around incremental loads, binding hydration, and repo-motion recovery
node.summary: Hydrate native plan graphs and continuity state incrementally, then rebind plan bindings through authored anchors, lineage, concept-centered recovery, and explicit unresolved states so plan intent survives repo motion without pretending runtime handles are authored truth.

acceptance.label: Workspace and session hydration rebuild native plan graphs, continuity state, and execution overlays incrementally rather than requiring whole-Prism snapshot reconstruction.
Plan binding hydration follows authored anchor, then lineage, then concept-centered recovery, then explicit unresolved handling during reload or repo motion.
Hydrated binding overlays and runtime handles stay runtime-only while published plan state continues to carry stable repo refs and authored bindings only.

node.title: Demote snapshots to compatibility caches, bootstrap accelerators, export artifacts, and plan compaction outputs
node.summary: Keep replay authoritative while allowing deterministic per-plan snapshots and compaction artifacts derived from canonical event history so snapshots remain secondary acceleration and export mechanisms rather than runtime truth.

acceptance.label: Deterministic per-plan snapshots or compaction artifacts are derived from canonical event history rather than acting as a separate source of truth.
Snapshots serve compatibility caching, bootstrap acceleration, and export use cases without regaining authority over native runtime plan state.
Snapshot and compaction flows preserve the native plan information needed for reload and inspection while remaining explicitly secondary to replay.

node.title: Validate reload latency, concurrency behavior, migration safety, and native plan persistence correctness
node.summary: Validate that the migrated persistence model preserves correctness for native plan graphs, binding hydration, compatibility projections, and concurrent reload or mutation behavior while keeping latency and migration risks acceptable.

acceptance.label: Validation covers reload latency and concurrency behavior for native plan graphs, continuity state, and incremental hydration paths.
Migration checks prove native plan persistence, compatibility projections, and binding hydration remain correct across reloads and upgrades.
The final validation pass exercises the new persistence boundaries without assuming snapshot authority or SQLite-specific orchestration.

node.title: Classify authoritative state vs derived artifacts across persistence, plans, and projections
node.summary: Define what is authoritative runtime truth versus derived or export-only state, including native plan graphs, authored node and edge state, execution overlays, compatibility projections, summaries, and snapshot artifacts.

acceptance.label: Persistence classification explicitly marks native plan graph data, authored node fields, authored edges, execution overlays, and stable published refs as authoritative state where appropriate.
The classification explicitly marks compatibility task projections, plan summaries and recommendations, snapshots, compaction artifacts, and other caches or exports as derived state.
The remaining persistence-migration tasks can reference this classification to avoid reintroducing dual truth between native plans and compatibility projections.

node.title: Introduce backend-neutral persistence and session abstractions for native plans and coordination state
node.summary: Create backend-neutral persistence and session interfaces that cover native plan event storage, snapshot loading, coordination continuity, and binding hydration so SQLite becomes one implementation rather than the architectural center.

acceptance.label: Shared persistence and session contracts cover native plan event storage, native plan snapshot loading, coordination continuity state, and binding hydration or rebinding without SQLite-specific assumptions.
SQLite-specific construction and runtime assumptions are pushed behind backend implementations or factories instead of leaking through shared orchestration paths.
A future Postgres backend can implement the same contracts for plans, memories, concepts, and coordination state without another architectural rewrite.

node.title: Introduce backend-neutral persistence and session abstractions for native plans and coordination state
node.summary: Create backend-neutral persistence and session interfaces that cover native plan event storage, snapshot loading, coordination continuity, and binding hydration so SQLite becomes one implementation rather than the architectural center.

acceptance.label: Shared persistence and session contracts explicitly distinguish repo-published truth in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state in the backend, and process-local ephemeral caches.
Backend-neutral interfaces cover native plan event storage, coordination continuity state, runtime overlays, and optional snapshot or compaction loaders without SQLite-specific assumptions.
A future Postgres backend can implement the same shared-runtime contracts for plans, memories, concepts, claims, handoffs, and coordination state without another architectural rewrite.

plan.title: Migrate PRISM persistence toward backend-neutral, DB-native authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state while preserving a three-plane model: repo-published truth in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state in the backend, and process-local ephemeral caches. Keep snapshots and plan compaction artifacts derived-only, and make native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, compatibility projection, and multi-instance runtime coordination explicit persistence concerns rather than a parallel track.
plan.goal: Migrate PRISM persistence toward backend-neutral, DB-native authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state while preserving a three-plane model: repo-published truth in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state in the backend, and process-local ephemeral caches. Keep snapshots and plan compaction artifacts derived-only, and make native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, compatibility projection, and multi-instance runtime coordination explicit persistence concerns rather than a parallel track.

node.title: Make native plan and coordination writes authoritative via incremental DB-native persistence
node.summary: Replace snapshot-shaped write authority with incremental native writes so plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through authoritative event-backed storage while compatibility projections become sinks rather than semantic write centers.

acceptance.label: Native plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through the authoritative DB-native write path rather than through snapshot-shaped rewrites.
The authoritative runtime write path supports revision-aware or compare-and-swap style mutation semantics and idempotent event handling suitable for multi-instance coordination.
Compatibility task or coordination projections are updated as import or export compatibility views and are no longer treated as the authoritative write truth.

node.title: Move outcomes, projections, and plan read models onto incremental maintenance
node.summary: Maintain outcomes, compatibility projections, plan summaries, and recommendation read models incrementally from authoritative events so plan-native state does not require full snapshot rebuilds to become queryable.

acceptance.label: Outcome history, compatibility projections, and plan-native read models such as summaries or next recommendations are maintained incrementally from authoritative events.
Shared runtime read models cover active claims, handoffs, maintenance queues, and live plan materializations without requiring whole-snapshot rebuilds after each mutation or reload.
Projection maintenance clearly distinguishes authoritative event streams from derived read models, exported artifacts, and process-local caches.

node.title: Refactor WorkspaceSession hydration around incremental loads, binding hydration, and repo-motion recovery
node.summary: Hydrate native plan graphs and continuity state incrementally, then rebind plan bindings through authored anchors, lineage, concept-centered recovery, and explicit unresolved states so plan intent survives repo motion without pretending runtime handles are authored truth.

acceptance.label: Workspace and session hydration rebuild native plan graphs, continuity state, and execution overlays incrementally rather than requiring whole-Prism snapshot reconstruction.
Plan binding hydration follows authored anchor, then lineage, then concept-centered recovery, then explicit unresolved handling during reload or repo motion.
Runtime hydration preserves cross-instance session continuity and keeps repo, branch, and worktree identity explicit so mutable runtime state does not bleed across unrelated workspaces.

node.title: Validate reload latency, concurrency behavior, migration safety, and native plan persistence correctness
node.summary: Validate that the migrated persistence model preserves correctness for native plan graphs, binding hydration, compatibility projections, and concurrent reload or mutation behavior while keeping latency and migration risks acceptable.

acceptance.label: Validation covers reload latency and concurrency behavior for native plan graphs, continuity state, leases, and incremental hydration paths across more than one runtime instance.
Migration checks prove native plan persistence, compatibility projections, binding hydration, and stale-session cleanup remain correct across reloads, upgrades, partial failures, and reconnects.

The final validation pass exercises optimistic concurrency, lease or heartbeat renewal, idempotent event replay, and repo or branch isolation without assuming snapshot authority or SQLite-specific orchestration.

plan.title: Migrate PRISM persistence toward authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state while preserving a three-plane model: repo-published truth in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state in local or remote backends, and process-local ephemeral caches. Make local embedded and shared remote runtime backends first-class deployment modes, support one Prism endpoint multiplexing multiple repo/worktree contexts through explicit repo, worktree, branch, session, and instance identity, and keep snapshots plus plan compaction artifacts derived-only while native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, compatibility projection, and multi-context coordination become explicit persistence concerns.
plan.goal: Migrate PRISM persistence toward authoritative runtime storage for memories, concepts, plans, and coordination state while preserving a three-plane model: repo-published truth in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state in local or remote backends, and process-local ephemeral caches. Make local embedded and shared remote runtime backends first-class deployment modes, support one Prism endpoint multiplexing multiple repo/worktree contexts through explicit repo, worktree, branch, session, and instance identity, and keep snapshots plus plan compaction artifacts derived-only while native plan graphs, bindings, hydration, rebinding, compatibility projection, and multi-context coordination become explicit persistence concerns.

node.title: Make native plan and coordination writes authoritative via incremental DB-native persistence
node.summary: Replace snapshot-shaped write authority with incremental native writes so plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through authoritative revisioned event-backed storage, carry explicit repo/worktree/branch/session/instance identity where scope depends on it, and remain suitable for both embedded and shared remote backends.

acceptance.label: Native plan, node, edge, and continuity mutations persist through an authoritative DB-native write path rather than through snapshot-shaped rewrites.
The authoritative runtime write path supports revision-aware or compare-and-swap semantics and idempotent event handling suitable for multi-instance coordination.
Authoritative writes record or preserve explicit repo, worktree, branch or checkout, session, and instance identity where runtime scoping depends on them.
Compatibility task or coordination projections remain import or export compatibility views and are not treated as the authoritative write truth.

node.title: Move outcomes, projections, and plan read models onto incremental maintenance
node.summary: Maintain outcomes, compatibility projections, plan summaries, recommendation frontiers, and shared runtime read models incrementally from authoritative events so one endpoint can serve many worktree contexts without relying on whole-snapshot rebuilds.

acceptance.label: Outcome history, compatibility projections, and plan-native read models such as summaries or next recommendations are maintained incrementally from authoritative events.

Shared runtime read models cover active claims, handoffs, maintenance queues, live plan materializations, and worktree-bound coordination state without requiring whole-snapshot rebuilds after each mutation or reload.
Read models support one endpoint serving many worktree contexts without leaking branch or worktree state across bound sessions.
Projection maintenance clearly distinguishes authoritative event streams from derived shared-runtime read models, exported artifacts, and process-local caches.

node.title: Refactor WorkspaceSession hydration around incremental loads, binding hydration, and repo-motion recovery
node.summary: Hydrate native plan graphs and continuity state incrementally, then rebind plan bindings and attach runtime overlays with explicit repo/worktree/branch identity so one Prism endpoint can host many worktree contexts cleanly.

acceptance.label: Workspace and session hydration rebuild native plan graphs, continuity state, and execution overlays incrementally rather than requiring whole-Prism snapshot reconstruction.
Plan binding hydration follows authored anchor, then lineage, then concept-centered recovery, then explicit unresolved handling during reload or repo motion.
Runtime hydration preserves cross-instance session continuity and keeps repo, branch, and worktree identity explicit so mutable runtime state does not bleed across unrelated workspaces.
Sessions can bind explicitly to a worktree context so one endpoint can host many worktrees without requiring one daemon per worktree.

node.title: Validate reload latency, concurrency behavior, migration safety, and native plan persistence correctness
node.summary: Validate that the migrated persistence model preserves correctness for native plan graphs, binding hydration, compatibility projections, shared-runtime coordination, and single-endpoint multi-worktree isolation while keeping latency and migration risks acceptable.

acceptance.label: Validation covers reload latency and concurrency behavior for native plan graphs, continuity state, leases, and incremental hydration paths across more than one runtime instance and more than one worktree context.

Migration checks prove native plan persistence, compatibility projections, binding hydration, stale-session cleanup, and worktree context isolation remain correct across reloads, upgrades, partial failures, and reconnects.

The final validation pass exercises optimistic concurrency, lease or heartbeat renewal, idempotent event replay, single-endpoint multi-worktree binding, and repo or branch isolation without assuming snapshot authority or SQLite-specific orchestration.
